questions.question: 1 (a) The table below gives information about particles. Complete the table. Define the term element. ................................................................................

parts.answer: Substance that cannot be broken down into anything simpler / substance that cannot be broken down (by chemical means) / substance containing atoms with the same atomic number or proton number;
number of protons number of neutrons number of electrons 31 38 31 31 40 31 M1 column one; M2 column two; M3 column three;
conducts electricity or heat / malleable / ductile / sonorous / shiny;

any two from: • (low) melting point / (low) boiling point; • hardness / softness / rubidium can be cut easily; • strength; • (low) density; Page 4 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0620 42 • bubbles / effervescence / fizzing; • flame / sparks / ignites; • movement; • dissolves / forms a solution / disappears / gets smaller; • floats; • rubidium melts / rubidium forms a ball; • explosion; O → 2RbOH + H formula of RbOH; whole equation completely correct; / Cs → Rb → K → Na → Li; / glasses / gloves / safety screen / stand at safe distance / tongs / open space; PO;

CO; solid; poor conductor / non-conductor; simple molecular / simple (covalent); Page 5 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0620 42 or bonds need a lot of energy or heat to break; or (there are) no weak bonds / no (weak) intermolecular forces; or weak intermolecular forces or weak van der Waals’ forces; or low amount of energy needed to break intermolecular / van der Waals’ forces; / no mobile or moving electrons / all electrons used in bonding / made of uncharged molecules;
